source_note: CONFIRMED: Brent ~$61 early 2026 before war risk priced in (EIA/Reuters: Brent rose $61->$72 over Jan-Feb on rising conflict risk). Used as P0 -- the but-for, no-closure counterfactual. Normal Hormuz flow ~20 mb/d total oil (EIA).
Brent mid-way through the Jan-Feb rise from $61 to $72 as conflict risk built. Daily value approximate.

CONFIRMED: Brent $72.48 on 28 Feb (Wikipedia, Economic impact of the 2026 Iran war). Operation Epic Fury begins; traffic falls ~70% within days. Production down 6.7 mb/d by 10 Mar, 10+ mb/d by 12 Mar.
Iran declared strait closed 4 Mar (CONFIRMED, Wikipedia/CRS R45281). Price INTERPOLATED between the 28 Feb and 8 Mar anchors.
CONFIRMED: Brent surpassed $100 on 8 Mar for the first time in four years (Wikipedia).
CONFIRMED: Brent $112.57 on 27 Mar, a 55% rise from 28 Feb (Wikipedia). IRGC declares strait closed to vessels to/from US, Israel, and allies.
CONFIRMED: Brent peaked at ~$126/bbl (Wikipedia); largest-ever monthly oil price increase in Mar 2026. Exact peak date approximate.
